idea: Starting persistent 1p2c FP16->FP32 kernel adapted to K=16416; established first custom kernel baseline at 181.49T (44.3% of cuBLAS 409.87T)
Switch 1p2c→1p1c WN=256 STAGES=2 TILE_M=64: reduces SMEM from 196KB to 144KB, fewer warp-groups, less register pressure → +15% to 209T
Switch persistent 114-CTA → non-persistent 16705-CTA (TILE_M=64, WN=256, STAGES=3): full GPU parallelism instead of serialized tile scheduling → +27% to 266T (64.8% cuBLAS)
STAGES=4 with WN=256 (224KB SMEM): slightly worse than STAGES=3 (184KB SMEM) — extra stage increases pressure without benefit at this shape
Phase 2: move empty[stage].arrive() before warpgroup_commit_batch (early arrive): signals producer to preload next tile while WGMMA completes → barrier stall reduction → +4.2% to 277T (67.5% cuBLAS)
warpgroup_wait<1> with extra final wait<0>: slight regression 277T→275T, extra drain overhead outweighs pipeline benefit at this configuration
STRUCTURAL 1p2c split-out WN=128: 3 warpgroups (384 threads) slower than 1p1c WN=256 (256 threads) — narrower N-tile reduces compute per TMA load, overhead dominates at 186T
TILE_K=128 via 2 TMA loads/barrier with STAGES=2: doubles WGMMA per sync but 2x TMA overhead and fewer pipeline stages hurt latency hiding, -12T vs iter005
Move warpgroup_arrive() before inner loop (1 call vs 4): compiler likely already hoists this — no gain at 275.7T vs 276.9T (-0.4%)
